# Transcrição: 05-Stranger in Panera

- **Fonte:** 05-Stranger in Panera
- **Duração:** 06:32
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Thank you. It's December 23rd. It's two days before Christmas and my wife has abandoned me. I'm trapped in mall traffic sitting behind the wheel and in the back seat of my car are truly two of the most despicable and annoying human beings I have ever met in my life. My children. Clara is six and Charlie is three and there are people in the world who will tell you these kids are brilliant beams of light but that is bullshit because they are just like my wife. They are spending their entire lives trying to slow me down and prevent me from doing the things that I want to do. These are children who literally stop and smell the roses. My wife takes them to flower shows because they ask to go to flower shows. They stop me in parking lots and say look dad and point to the clouds and say that cloud looks like a teapot. Take a picture. And I think I just want to get in the store. And they talk to everyone. They talk to random strangers all the time. They are dangerously friendly children. And my wife loves babies and she's given this to my daughter so everywhere we go my daughter stops mothers with infants and she has conversations with them like what's the name of your baby and how old is the baby and breast or bottle feeding. She's six years old and she's just talking to everyone. And I'm sick of it and on this day it is the worst day to slow me down because it's December 23rd. I have to buy Christmas presents because I haven't bought a single present and I am beyond the Amazon Prime click and save your ass date. So I actually have to go into a physical store and look at shit on shelves to determine which shit belongs to which person and what will look good in wrapping paper and I'm angry. This day is going bad for me and my wife had the nerve on this day to get a haircut. And when she goes to get a haircut it is a three hour ordeal. She is a woman with short black hair. All she gets is a trim. The trimmiest of trims. A trim I don't even notice most of the time but it takes three hours because she begins the process with someone who says what are your goals for today. That is shit that I cannot abide by. And so we're going to the mall but we can't go to the mall because my kids are hungry and we have to be outside because like my wife no one wants to ever be in the house but me I make enormous mortgage payments for a home nobody wants to spend a moment in. So we go to Panera and when we go into Panera I know right away they've screwed up the floor because they have the brown tiles except in an occasional red tile and if you have kids you know that means your kids are just going to leap from one red tile to the other and they're going to knock people over and land grilled cheese on the ground. So I say go to the dining room and find a booth and I know they're going to kill each

## [03:01 - 06:06]

other while fighting over it but I don't care because they're not with me right now. So I go to the cashier and she's new I can tell because she's nervous and so now I'm nervous and I start giving her the order and I say listen my daughter is allergic to peanuts can you please note it on the order and she looks and she says well you didn't order anything with peanuts. I say I know but could you just note it because she's allergic and it's a good idea and she says well you really didn't order anything with peanuts and what I want to say to her is listen lady there is a substance in this world that's basically in every food as far as I can tell that will kill my daughter if she eats it so could you please just fucking put peanuts on the order but I don't do that because I'm a better person than that. So I charge past her I go past the counter and into the employee area I take two steps into the kitchen and I go listen you're going to make a grilled cheese and when you do and then the manager's right in front of me and she says sir and I say no you don't understand your cashier will not put peanuts on the order and my daughter's allergic and she's not going to die today and the manager to her everlasting credit softens and she says sir I understand and I will take care of it but you need to get out of my kitchen right now and she's right I'm crazy and the kids have made me crazy my wife has made me crazy I get out of the kitchen she finishes my order I go to the dining room to find my kids and I cannot believe what I see they have chosen a booth in the back Clara is on one side Charlie is on the other side and in between there is a woman who I do not recognize and an infant they're sitting with strangers and I'm thinking how am I going to solve this problem but Clara knows what I'm gonna do because she says it's okay dad she said we can sit with them and have lunch this is Hannah and she points to the infant and so I look at the woman and she looks at me she gives me that sign that I know this is ridiculous but let's do this and so I sit down with a stranger at Panera and Clara introduces me to them and we make some chit chat and then she asks the woman she says where's Hannah's daddy today and the woman says oh Hannah doesn't have a daddy and my wife and Clara says oh is it are you a mommy mommy family and she says no it's just Hannah and me and that's the moment when I look at this woman for the first time and I really see her and I see the bags under her eyes and the slumping shoulders and I see the the stuff that she has everywhere she's got the stroller and the car seat and the diapers and all the plastic paraphernalia that we need to keep kids alive today the pacifiers and the bottles all stuff that I haven't seen in a little while because my kids are older so first time I really see this woman who's taking care of this baby and all of this stuff by herself and I'm so angry at Clara because she has shown me the thing that I need to see in this moment that I'm wandering this world complaining because my wife left me with my kids for three hours three stupid hours that I am complaining about I remember when Clara was

## [06:06 - 06:32]

born I thought we needed seven adults to keep her alive and this woman is doing it on her own and she's smiling and she is chatting with my daughter in a way I have not chatted with her all day and I sit there with these two brilliant beams of light knowing I am never going to be as beautiful and as wonderful as they are thank you
